extraction.one_line_summary: VERNON Ethan won the sprint after ALMEIDA João gained one second over QUINTANA Nairo at the intermediate sprint 17km from finish to take the overall lead
tactical_patterns: ALMEIDA contested intermediate sprint 17km from finish to gain one second and overall lead, UAE abandoned sprinter support.
JOHANNESSEN Tobias Halland contested intermediate sprint for GC time gaps despite not being top-10 overall, pressuring joint leaders.
CARAPAZ Richard attacked on flat stage with minimal elevation after two mountain days, exploiting depleted sprinter rosters.
GROVES Kaden positioned by BikeExchange through final corners despite DENNIS Rohan doing early leadout, fragmented sprint trains.

rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: positive: tactically astute to contest intermediate sprint for GC lead rather than relying on team to control finale
rider_signals.VERNON Ethan: positive: upgraded fourth place from Stage 2 to victory, timed sprint perfectly on slight ramp finish after 5h20m
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: caught out at crucial intermediate sprint 17km from finish, losing overall lead by single second
rider_signals.BAUHAUS Phil: neutral: led through final roundabouts solo after team fragmented, held second place without leadout support
rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: neutral: pulled early for DEKKER David who never appeared, INEOS sprint train collapsed before finale
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: neutral: attempted long-range attack on flat stage suggesting limited confidence in sprint outcome
rider_signals.VAN WILDER Ilan: negative: crashed at late roundabout after contact with GALVÁN Francisco, QuickStep lost support rider before victory
rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: neutral: well-positioned by BikeExchange through corners but faded to fifth, lacked final acceleration
rider_signals.HOFSTETTER Hugo: negative: effective leadout positioning turned to ninth place fade, no acceleration after 206km effort
